verses.text: The fifth trumpet: a star falls with the key of 'the bottomless pit,' and smoke darkens the sun and air. Pit opened. Hell's smoke pours out at the trumpet.
The fifth trumpet: a star falls with the key of 'the bottomless pit,' and smoke darkens the sun and air. Pit opened. Hell's smoke pours out at the trumpet.
Locusts come to torment, not kill, those without God's seal; men 'shall seek death, and shall not find it.' Tormenting. The unsealed suffer and long in vain to die.
Locusts come to torment, not kill, those without God's seal; men 'shall seek death, and shall not find it.' Tormenting. The unsealed suffer and long in vain to die.
Locusts come to torment, not kill, those without God's seal; men 'shall seek death, and shall not find it.' Tormenting. The unsealed suffer and long in vain to die.
Locusts come to torment, not kill, those without God's seal; men 'shall seek death, and shall not find it.' Tormenting. The unsealed suffer and long in vain to die.
The locusts were like horses prepared for battle, crowned, with faces of men and 'teeth... as the teeth of lions.' Dreadful host. A terrifying army is loosed.
The locusts were like horses prepared for battle, crowned, with faces of men and 'teeth... as the teeth of lions.' Dreadful host. A terrifying army is loosed.
The locusts were like horses prepared for battle, crowned, with faces of men and 'teeth... as the teeth of lions.' Dreadful host. A terrifying army is loosed.
They have stings like scorpions, a king over them, 'Abaddon'; 'one woe is past; and... there come two woes more.' First woe done. Worse remains for the earth.
They have stings like scorpions, a king over them, 'Abaddon'; 'one woe is past; and... there come two woes more.' First woe done. Worse remains for the earth.
They have stings like scorpions, a king over them, 'Abaddon'; 'one woe is past; and... there come two woes more.' First woe done. Worse remains for the earth.
The sixth trumpet: four angels 'bound in the great river Euphrates' are loosed 'to slay the third part of men.' Loosed angels. Vast destruction is prepared and timed.
The sixth trumpet: four angels 'bound in the great river Euphrates' are loosed 'to slay the third part of men.' Loosed angels. Vast destruction is prepared and timed.
The sixth trumpet: four angels 'bound in the great river Euphrates' are loosed 'to slay the third part of men.' Loosed angels. Vast destruction is prepared and timed.
An army of two hundred million horsemen; by fire, smoke, and brimstone 'the third part of men killed.' Their power is in mouth and tail. Slaughter. A third of mankind perishes.

An army of two hundred million horsemen; by fire, smoke, and brimstone 'the third part of men killed.' Their power is in mouth and tail. Slaughter. A third of mankind perishes.
The rest, not killed, 'repented not' of their idols, murders, sorceries, nor thefts. Hard hearts. Even plagues do not turn the impenitent.
